texts.content:
Local Bakery Wins National Award

A family-owned bakery in downtown Springfield has been named 'Best Artisan Bakery of the Year' at the National Food Industry Awards. Sweet Rise Bakery, which opened its doors in 2012, was recognized for its sourdough bread and seasonal fruit pastries.

Owner Maria Gonzalez said the award was a surprise. 'We just focus on making quality bread with local ingredients,' she told reporters. The bakery uses organic flour from farms within 50 miles of the city and maintains traditional fermentation methods that take up to 48 hours per batch.

The award ceremony took place in Chicago on Monday evening. As part of the prize, Sweet Rise will receive a $10,000 grant and a feature in Food Culture Magazine.

questions.stem: Why did Sweet Rise Bakery win the award?
options: A. It has the most locations in the state
B. It uses traditional methods and local ingredients
C. It has been in business longer than any other bakery
D. It won a regional competition in Chicago
questions.explanation: The article highlights their use of 'organic flour from farms within 50 miles' and 'traditional fermentation methods' as the basis for the recognition.

questions.stem: What does Maria Gonzalez's response suggest about her attitude toward the award?
options: A. She expected to win because of her high standards
B. She was modest and somewhat surprised
C. She was disappointed it took so long to be recognized
D. She plans to expand the business immediately
questions.explanation: Gonzalez said the award 'was a surprise' and focused on simply 'making quality bread,' suggesting a humble and genuine attitude.

questions.stem: What will the bakery receive as part of the award?
options: A. A feature in a national newspaper
B. A cash prize and a magazine feature
C. A contract to supply hotels in Chicago
D. Free equipment for their kitchen
questions.explanation: The article states the bakery will receive 'a $10,000 grant and a feature in Food Culture Magazine.'

texts.content:
City Public Library — New Membership Application

Please complete all fields marked with *.

Full Name:* ________________________
Date of Birth:* ____ / ____ / ________
Email Address:* ________________________
Phone Number: ________________________

Address:*
Street: ________________________
City: ________________________ ZIP: ________

Identification Required: Please bring a valid government-issued photo ID and a proof of current address (utility bill, bank statement, or lease agreement).

Membership Type (choose one):
☐ Standard (up to 10 items, 3-week loan period)
☐ Premium ($25/year, up to 25 items, 6-week loan period)

I agree to the library's borrowing terms and conditions.
Signature: ________________________ Date: ________

questions.stem: Which of the following is NOT listed as acceptable proof of address?
options: A. A utility bill
B. A bank statement
C. A lease agreement
D. A pay stub
questions.explanation: The form lists 'utility bill, bank statement, or lease agreement' as acceptable proof of address, but does not mention a pay stub.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the Premium membership?
options: A. It is available only to city residents
B. It allows more items and a longer borrowing period for a fee
C. It includes free access to digital resources
D. It requires a separate application form
questions.explanation: The Premium membership costs $25/year and allows 'up to 25 items' with a '6-week loan period,' compared to the Standard's 10 items and 3 weeks.
